examples: The power of diligence can scatter mental defilement.
To free from dirt, defilement, or guilt; purge or clean.
Our defilement must be gotten rid, of or we will not be ready.
It felt like a collective defilement, a dance atop this concrete tomb of a basketball arena.
But did it upset Condo to have his paintings treated in a manner that some fine artists might consider defilement twice over?
